May in review. Strategy reset for $100MM.

Where We Landed · May 1–31, 2026

May at a glance.

May was the month the funnel proved itself. Cost per click hit a 12-month low at $0.36, nine paid leads came through, and Randy · SS Taxation emerged as the clear winner — driving 3 of the 9 leads on a single ad. The data validates the persona pivot we're rolling out in June.

Reporting window: May 1 → May 31, 2026 · Source: Meta Ads Manager export (ad-level totals) + landing-page form attribution. Total May spend was $538.44: $404.87 on the established 6-ad cohort plus $133.57 on new test campaigns launched mid-month.

Significantly below benchmark

The headline: May delivered the most efficient month on record — $0.36 CPC, 8.0% CTR, and a real cost-per-lead of $59.83. Randy · SS Taxation is the clear ad-level winner, and Social Security & retirement tax content is what's converting at the asset level. Pete & Pam life insurance generated the cheapest clicks but only 1 lead from 304 clicks — the data behind the insurance pivot. This is what validated the strategic recalibration on May 27.

Paid Media · May 1–31, 2026

Per-ad performance.

Six established ads ran across May plus new mid-month test campaigns. Randy · SS Taxation was the standout — converting at $37 per Meta-attributed lead and delivering 3 of 9 form leads. Pete & Pam Free Quote drove the most clicks at the lowest CPC but produced just 1 lead from 304 clicks — the data behind the insurance pivot.

May paid media · all ads combined Established 6-ad cohort + new test campaigns · Targeting recalibrated to women 65+, men 65+

SS Taxation PDF is the proven magnet — 44% of leads downloaded it.

Insights · Lead-Level Data

What the lead data tells us.

Retirees dominate at 78%. Seven of nine leads carry the retired-professional persona tag — strong confirmation that the May 27 pivot to HNW Retirees + Ma & Pa Miller is the right call.

The "EduSavings" LP is actually a retiree-magnet. All 4 leads that converted on the Pete/Pam EduSavings landing page were tagged retired-professional, not protecting-parents. Grandparents helping with grandchildren's education — not young families — are the audience. Rename and reposition this LP as a retiree resource.

One ad campaign carried 8 of 9 leads. Campaign 6910825202044 (the cold March 2026 audience) drove 89% of conversions; the Pete/Pam Conversions campaign produced just 1. The retiree audience definition inside that one campaign is the volume engine — protect it and replicate it.

The SS Taxation PDF is the only magnet that converted. Four of nine leads downloaded it; the other five filled a form with no magnet attached. Of the lead magnets in rotation, SS Taxation is the proven one — lead with it in Funnel A and Funnel C education touchpoints.

The headline: The audience and the magnet are settled — retirees + SS Taxation content. The recalibration to HNW Retirees + Ma & Pa Miller is supported at every level of the data.

Beyond the Form · Engaged Audience

9 form leads — and ~470 more in the warm pool around them.

The 9 form fills are the leads we can hand to advisors today. The engaged audience around them splits into two pools: a small DM-able group (people who reacted to organic posts — identifiable through Meta) and a much larger retargetable group (paid clickers who left the platform and are anonymous to us but reachable through Custom Audience). Both are warm; each needs a different motion.

Tier 1 · Identified
9
Form leads. Confirmed — name, email, phone. Ready for advisor outreach today.
Tier 2 · DM-able
31
Reactions on organic posts. All likes — zero comments, zero shares across 17 May posts. Likely 15–25 unique individuals after de-dupe.
Tier 3 · Retargetable
~431
Paid clickers — anonymous to us. 440 ad clicks minus 9 form leads. Reachable via Meta Custom Audience, not direct DM.
Total warm pool
~471
Two paths to activate — DM the small organic engager pool, retarget the much larger anonymous clicker pool.
Paid engagement — May

From 3,734 unique people reached.

5,470
Total impressions
3,734
Unique reach
~440
Link clicks
9
Form leads
The 2% click→form rate is in line with cold-paid benchmarks. The other 431 link-clickers showed interest strong enough to leave the platform — they're warm, just not identified. Because they clicked off Meta, we don't have their usernames; they activate through retargeting (path B below), not DM. The ad posts themselves also produced reactions and comments we can see — that smaller pool counts toward the DM-able 200+ in Tier 2.
Organic engagement — May (verified via MBS export)

Across 17 organic posts.

17
Posts published
31
Reactions · likes
0
Comments + shares
6
Link clicks
Top organic post (May 26 · "Retiring from a business" at 37.5% interaction) and the SS $6,000 Senior Bonus post (May 5 · 33.3%) drove most of the engagement. Zero comments and zero shares across the full month — every engagement action is a passive reaction. The DM motion has a real but small input pool: ~15–25 unique individuals after de-duplication. The June fix is to invite replies (questions, polls, "tell us in the comments" CTAs) so future months produce active engagement signals the DM motion can hook into.
Path A · DM Motion

Activating Tier 2 — the ~15–25 DM-able engagers.

Organic post reactors with visible Meta identities. Small pool today; the topic match matters more than volume.
01
Pull the engager list
Weekly Meta Business Suite export of likers, commenters, share-ers, new follows — across organic posts and ad posts.
02
Match person to LP
Educational engager → LP-01 White Paper. New follower → LP-03 VSL. "Ready to talk" commenter → LP-02 Qualifier.
03
Personalized DM
Short, named, references the specific post they engaged with — plus the matched LP link. No mass-DM scripts.
Path B · Retargeting Motion

Activating Tier 3 — the ~431 paid clickers.

For people who clicked an ad and visited an LP but didn't convert. Anonymous to us — reachable only through Meta's retargeting tools.
01
Build the Custom Audience
Pixel-based audience of LP visitors who didn't fill the form — last 30 / 90 / 180 days. Refreshes automatically as new clicks come in.
02
Step up the CTA
First touch sent them to LP-03 (VSL) or LP-01 (paper). The retarget moves them to LP-02 hot qualifier — higher intent ask, shorter path to a call.
03
Lookalike off the converted 9
Use the 9 form leads as the seed for a 1–3% lookalike — finds new cold prospects who match the retirees who actually converted.

Why this matters: Even with the smaller DM pool, a 20–30% conversion of the ~15–25 engagers (because they're highly topic-matched) plus a 2% retarget-to-form conversion on the ~431 paid clickers nets ~12–18 additional warm leads per month — more than doubling the form-fill volume, at zero additional ad budget. The bigger lever is fixing organic engagement quality: invite replies in June so the DM-able pool grows from 25 to 100+.

Audience

Where the spend converted.

May's audience data is unambiguous: women 65+ are the converting segment. They received the most spend, the most reach, and were the only segment to produce attributed leads. This is the data that informed the persona pivot to retirees.

Organic Social · May 1–31, 2026

Organic posting — the topical signal is clear, the reach is small.

17 posts published across May. The data is unambiguous about which topics resonate (business owner retirement and Social Security) and which don't (college savings and life insurance for young families). The reach numbers are small — typical for a growing page — and engagement was entirely reactions; the page had zero comments and zero shares all month, which sharpens what the DM and content motion needs to do next.

Posts published
17
Across 31 days · ~4/week
Top post
37.5%
Business owner retirement · May 26
Strong topic
33.3%
SS $6,000 Senior Bonus · May 5
Total engagement
31
Reactions · 0 comments · 0 shares
May 26 · Top performer 37.5% interaction

"Retiring from a business can create several income sources at once..." — the highest-engaging post of the month. Business owner retirement transition resonates with the recalibrated HNW Retiree + Ma & Pa Miller personas.

3
reactions / 8 reach
2
clicks
May 5 · Strong performer 33.3% interaction

"If you're approaching retirement, the math on Social Security has shifted this year..." — second-highest of the month and the topic that also converted on paid (SS Taxation PDF generated 4 of 9 paid leads).

3
reactions / 9 reach
0
clicks
May 2 · Highest reach

"If you're approaching retirement, one of the biggest decisions you'll make is when to claim Social Security..." — reached 18 people (the most of any May post) and drove the most link clicks (2). Stat-driven SS-claiming-mistakes content — topic resonates; reach is the constraint.

4
reactions / 18 reach
7
total clicks
May 18 · Underperformer

"If you have young kids, college may feel far away. But education costs have a way of..." — zero engagement, the only May post with 0% interaction rate. Combined with zero paid conversions on the protecting-parents persona, this confirms the deprioritization.

0
reactions / 9 reach
0
clicks

Organic takeaways: The topical signal is consistent across organic and paid — business owner retirement (37.5%) and Social Security (33.3%) are the winning topics; college savings and life-insurance-for-young-families are the underperformers (0% on May 18). The harder finding is what didn't happen: zero comments and zero shares across all 17 posts. Engagement is entirely passive reactions, which limits the DM motion's input pool. June pivot: reduce to 3 posts/week, concentrate exclusively on the two winning topics, and rework post copy to invite a reply rather than a reaction — direct questions, "tell us in the comments," polls, and CTA-led prompts. Reach (~10/post average) is the bigger constraint than topic mix; the path to fixing it is paid boosting on the winning topics and content patterns that earn shares.

Trends — May

What's working.

Three signals are now crystal clear after May — they shape everything that follows.

Audience confirmed
Women 65+ convert

SS / retirement tax
4 of 9 leads · 19–27% organic interaction
Hook style confirmed
Specific over generic
"$6,000 Senior Bonus" > "retirement planning"

Momentum heading into June: The funnel is converting, the audience is identified, and the content direction is proven. May's job was to validate the foundation Q1 built — and it did. The path forward (Part 2) takes these signals and turns them into the engine that drives toward $100MM AUM by year-end.

Part 2 — Path Forward

May validated the model. June activates it.

The data above proved which audiences convert, which content works, and where to stop. The path forward turns that picture into the engine that drives Luxon Brown toward $100MM AUM by year-end.

The Strategic Shift

Three resets defining the path forward.

On May 27 the Luxon Brown team reset around a single goal: $100MM AUM by year-end. That goal reshapes everything — the persona focus, the acquisition channel, and what SuperSymm's role is heading into Q3.

Where AUM stands today

$70–75MM today · $100MM is the year-end target.

~$25MM
Retail · Ma & Pa Miller
$45.5MM+
Existing retail client base
401(k) side
~$25–30MM
The balance of LB's book
Six-month delta to $100MM
~$25MM
The bridge to year-end goal
The Jetpack math: Advisor Jetpack guarantees 30 qualified appointments with $3MM+ manageable assets over 4 months — $90MM of pipeline met with. Even a conservative 20% close rate yields ~$18MM. The rest comes from the SuperSymm remarketing engine (seminar list, dormant leads, existing client cross-sell) — the focus of this 90-day plan.
Primary goal

$100MM AUM by year-end

From ~$70–75MM today to $100MM is the run. The milestone unlocks SEC registration and removes Arkansas-level overhead. 30+ qualified appointments / $3MM+ each is the path.

Acquisition

Advisor Jetpack takes the lead

Direct appointment generation with guaranteed minimums. Launches July (Signal loan approved). Significant paid spend reallocates to Jetpack.

SuperSymm role

From acquisition to conversion

Shift focus from lead generation to nurturing and converting the existing lead base + Jetpack hand-offs. The funnel infrastructure becomes the differentiator.

Personas · Recalibrated

Two primaries. Everything else, secondary.

The recalibration narrowed the ideal client profile. P1 and P1B drive the strategy from June forward; growth markets sit in the wings until the primaries are humming.

P1 · Priority 1

HNW Retirees

Age
60–80
Liquid assets
$1M+ (net worth $2.5M+)
Background
Retired professionals or inactive business owners
Financial goals
Asset optimization, tax efficiency, legacy planning
Why ideal
Have assets, want to pay for guidance, need optimization
P1B · Core market

Ma & Pa Miller

Age
55–70
Liquid assets
$500K+
Background
25+ years at one company · government workers, educators, firefighters
Financial goals
401k rollover, Social Security optimization, retirement income
Why ideal
Transition moment — they need guidance and they're ready to engage
Secondary · Growth markets (on hold)
  • Pre-Retiree Paul — pause active campaigns; revisit once primaries are scaling
  • Arkansas Business Owner Betty — keep landing page and select content; pause paid
  • Divorced & Independent Diana — defined but not actively targeted
  • NextGen Investor Nancy — defined but not actively targeted
Deprioritized — content stopped
  • Pete & Pam (life insurance) — zero conversions from $46+ in May ads despite cheap $0.15 CPC. Off-persona. All life-insurance content retired.
  • Education savings / 529 — off-persona for retirees. Stopped.
Content Themes

Monthly themes — June through September.

Four themes anchor the next four months. Each maps to a primary persona, a proven lead magnet, and a specific landing page. Theme 1 is the worked example below.

Theme Topic Persona Lead magnet
1 Social Security & retirement tax P1B · Ma & Pa Miller SS Taxation PDF (reused)
2 Business owner retirement transition P1 · HNW Retirees Business owner retirement guide (new)
3 401k rollover & income planning P1B · Ma & Pa Miller 401k rollover checklist (new)
4 Retirement readiness & tools P1 + P1B · Both primaries Retirement calculator + roadmap wizard
1↓ Theme 1 in action — worked example Placeholder · content TBD
June rollout structure: social posts schedule, landing page refresh, email sequence trigger, lead-magnet promo — concrete plan to be filled in as creative and copy are produced.
 P1B · SS Taxation focus
Social Posting

Diversifying the social posting.

May's 5-posts-per-week pace dropped views/post by 54%. June drops to 3 posts/week and widens the mix of post types we cycle through — no single format every week, multiple ways into the same audience. Plus an active engagement layer that converts passive scrolling into direct outreach.

01
VSL clips
Short clips from the Brandon video — talking-head, on-message, 30–60 seconds.
Why: Faces build trust faster than text on cold audiences. Brandon's expertise comes through better in motion than in a static graphic.
02
Whitepaper & lead magnet promos
Direct-promo posts pointing to landing pages with downloadable guides (SS Taxation PDF, biz owner guide, 401k checklist).
Why: Captures identified leads straight from social. The SS Taxation PDF alone generated 4 of 9 May leads — there's clear demand to monetize.
03
Team highlights & culture
Posts featuring Chrissy, Gavin, Linda, Brandon, Taylor — quotes, day-in-the-life, milestones.
Why: Wealth management is a relationship business. Audiences buy from people they recognize, and the team needs faces on the feed.
04
"Schedule a call" direct CTA
Single-purpose posts that skip education and go straight to the booking link.
Why: Not every viewer needs to be nurtured — some are ready right now. A direct CTA surfaces them.
05
Educational posts
Stat-driven, hook-led content on the active monthly theme (SS optimization, retirement tax, 401k rollover).
Why: Still the engagement backbone — 19–27% interaction rates in May. The proven path needs to stay in the mix.
06
Active engagement (DMs)
Engaged followers — likers, commenters, savers — get a relevant DM with a direct LP link (VSL, whitepaper, or scheduler).
Why: Closes the loop on the warmest signal. Passive scroll → personalized DM → identified lead.
90-Day Plan

Phased rollout — June through September.

Three phases, sequenced. Phase 1 activates the existing lead pool through email-led funnels (click any flow below to expand). Phase 2 deepens the funnel; Phase 3 scales with tools and the podcast pilot.

Phase 1 — Activate the lead pool

Early · Weeks 1–2

Goal: Reactivate the existing lead pool through three email-led funnels — 30 emails total across A, B (3 sub-flows), and C (3 sub-flows) — all converging on 3 shared landing pages (VSL, white paper, hot qualifier) + the booking page. Email is the primary delivery; each flow ends in an urgency-form qualifier before booking.

Supporting work alongside the funnels
  • 30-email production set — Funnel A (4 emails · prospect nurture), Funnel B (12 emails across 3 sub-flows · seminar retargeting by attendee status), Funnel C (14 emails across 3 sub-flows · existing client upsell/cross-sell/relationship)
  • 3 shared landing pages — one VSL, one lead-magnet white paper, one hot-qualifier form. Every email CTA routes to one of the three (or to the booking page). All built with the Luxon Brown Institutional Warmth design system & FINRA-compliant copy.
  • Urgency-form qualifier — the LP-02 hot qualifier form is the single hot-lead capture across every funnel (Brandon/Gavin/Taylor calls a "yes" today)
  • Compliance-routed copy — Comply submission for tax/investment language; standard-disclaimer fast-track for relationship touchpoints
  • Social pivot — 3 posts/week (down from 5), synced to the active monthly theme so paid + organic + email reinforce one another
  • Lead magnets in rotation — SS Taxation PDF (proven · 4 of 9 May leads), Business Owner Retirement guide, 401k Rollover checklist, Life Insurance whitepaper (C1 only)
The 3 shared landing pages

Every email CTA in Phase 1 lands on one of these three pages (or the booking page). Click any LP node in the flows below to preview the page itself.

LP-03 · VSL
Video Sales Landing Page
Video-first intro to the firm + the 5 key retirement planning decisions. The trust-builder — used as the first destination for cold prospects, no-show re-engagement, and waitlist intros. Drives toward a free consultation booking.
Used by 7 emailsA-01 · B1-01 · B2-01 · B3-01 · C1-01 · C1-03 · C2-01
LP-01 · White Paper
Retirement Planning Guide Download
The lead magnet destination — delivers the white paper in exchange for name, email, and phone. The value drop that builds authority and gives a meaningful takeaway. Highest-intent capture page in the system.
Used by 7 emailsA-02 · B1-02 · B2-02 · B3-02 · C1-02 · C2-02 · C3-03
LP-02 · Hot Qualifier
Hot Lead Qualifier Form
Multi-section qualifier capturing life events, financial snapshot, and advisor status. Identifies high-intent contacts so advisors can prioritize who to call today. Hand-off point into Wealthbox.
Used by 6 emailsA-03 · B1-03 · B2-03 · B3-04 · C1-04 · C2-03
The flows — click any step to see the email or landing page

Each flow alternates email → destination. The larger pills are emails; the colored pills are the landing pages they link to. Click any step to preview.

Funnel A
Prospect nurture flow
Entry via social post or paid ad → opt-in → email nurture → landing page → booking. 4 emails routing to VSL, white paper, qualifier, then booking.
Welcome→ LP-03 VSL→ Lead magnet→ LP-01 Paper→ Hot qualifier→ LP-02 Qualifier→ Booking→ Book appt
Funnel B
Seminar retargeting · 3 sub-flows by attendee status
Three sub-flows segment past seminar contacts by what they did — and meet each with the right next touch. 12 emails total. Click any step to see the email or LP.
B1 · Registered, didn't show
High intent, low follow-through. Lead them back in with what they missed.
Missed you→ LP-03 VSL→ Lead magnet→ LP-01 Paper→ Hot qualifier→ LP-02 Qualifier→ Next seminar→ Book seat
B2 · Waitlisted (never seated)
Wanted in, didn't get a seat. Introduce the firm and earn their seat next time.
Intro VSL→ LP-03 VSL→ Seminar PDF→ LP-01 Paper→ Hot qualifier→ LP-02 Qualifier→ Next seminar→ Book seat
B3 · Attended the seminar
Saw the content, still deciding. Reinforce, invite to consult, and qualify.
Thank you→ LP-03 VSL→ Lead magnet→ LP-01 Paper→ Consultation→ Book consult→ Hot qualifier→ LP-02 Qualifier
Funnel C
Existing client — upsell, cross-sell & relationship deepening
Three sub-flows targeting existing clients by service profile: insurance-only clients not yet on AUM (C1), AUM clients with assets held elsewhere (C2), and ongoing relationship touchpoints — referrals, annual reviews, education, seasonal (C3). 14 emails total.
C1 · Insurance client — not yet on AUM
Educate insurance-only clients and move toward a wealth management conversation.
Insurance intro→ LP-03 VSL→ AUM education→ LP-01 Paper→ Social proof→ LP-03 VSL→ Hot qualifier→ LP-02 Qualifier→ Portfolio review→ Book review
C2 · AUM client — assets held elsewhere
Make the case for consolidation and unified planning across all accounts.
Consolidation→ LP-03 VSL→ Education→ LP-01 Paper→ Hot qualifier→ LP-02 Qualifier→ Review booking→ Book review
C3 · Relationship deepening — all existing clients
Ongoing touchpoints: referrals, annual reviews, quarterly education, seasonal, and market check-ins. Mixed destinations — only one routes to an LP; the rest go to booking or are pure relationship sends.
Referral→ Compliance review→ Annual review→ Book review→ Education→ LP-01 Paper→ Holiday→ Year-end review→ Market check-in→ Book check-in

Phase 2 — Deepen the funnel

Mid · Weeks 3–6

Goal: Build the assets that strengthen Phase 1 sequences and open up new lead segments — both prospects and existing clients.

Brandon video landing page (hub)
A single polished destination for the Brandon video that runs throughout every Phase 1 sequence. Lifts perceived professionalism and gives every email, post, and DM a place to land.
Referral + annual review campaign
A 3-email sequence triggered around each client's annual review. Existing clients are the firm's best lead source — this surfaces referrals at the moment trust is highest, and reinforces relationship health.
Cold lead re-engagement
A wake-up sequence for existing contacts dormant 6+ months. Zero acquisition cost, surfaces buyers who weren't ready before but are now. The existing list is an underused asset.
Polished client email templates
Standardize the onboarding, cross-sell, and annual-review emails currently varying by advisor. Templates raise quality, save advisor time, reinforce the brand, and become the backbone of Funnel C.
Blog post creation
A monthly cadence of long-form posts on the converting topics — Social Security taxation, 401k rollover, retirement income strategy. Each post anchors a landing page, supplies the underlying content for snippets that feed Funnel A & C emails and organic social, and builds search authority for LB's domain. Compliance-routed; aligned to the monthly theme.

Phase 3 — Scale

Later · Weeks 7–12

Goal: Convert proven sequences into self-serve tools and brand assets that scale beyond one-to-one advisor touches — so growth doesn't require linearly more advisor time.

Retirement calculator deploy
A self-serve calculator drives top-of-funnel engagement without advisor time. POC already exists; needs Brandon validation pass and finalized UX before deploy. Doubles as a lead capture mechanism.
Retirement roadmap wizard
Digitize the existing LB workbook into an interactive lead magnet. Captures qualified leads while educating prospects on what working with LB actually looks like — pre-frames the sales conversation.
Podcast pilot (2–3 episodes)
Long-form audio builds trust at depth — and gives LB shareable assets for social, email, and DMs. Pilot first to test format and reception before committing to a full season.
Fall seminar system ready
Q4 seminar prep starts in Phase 3 so the full system — registration LPs, email sequences, retargeting flows from Phase 1 — is live and ready when fall seminars run.
Asset Inventory

What we reuse, create, refine, retire.

May's data made the prioritization easy. Proven assets carry into June; off-persona assets are stopped; new asset creation focuses on the two winning topics.

Asset Status Notes
SS Taxation PDFReuseProven: 4 of 9 May leads. Slot into nurture sequence.
Randy landing pageReuse3 May leads. Update copy for retirement tax focus.
Betty landing pageReuse1 May lead. Keep structure, update copy.
Brandon video introCreate2–3 min on-camera intro. Anchor for all sequences.
Business owner retirement guideCreateNew PDF. Highest organic engagement (27%).
401k rollover checklistCreateNew PDF. Core P1B pain point.
Polished client email templatesCreateOnboarding, cross-sell, annual review — Phase 2.
Retirement calculatorRefinePOC exists. Needs Brandon validation before deploy.
Retirement roadmap wizardBuildDigitize existing LB workbook. Phase 3.
Insurance lead magnets / LPsRetireZero real conversions. Deprioritized persona.
Education savings PDFs / LPsRetireOff-persona. Stopped.
Working Rhythms

Process ahead.

The cadence that gets us from where we are to $100MM AUM.

Cadence

  • Bi-weekly reviews continue — performance + strategic alignment
  • Compliance two-tier — Fast Track for low-risk, Full Review for regulated content
  • Monthly performance reports with this same Editorial Brief format
  • Quarterly strategic planning — next big review at end of Q3

Next steps

  • Align on leads capture & notification — agree on how form leads and hot leads flow from SuperSymm into Wealthbox, and how Brandon, Gavin, and Taylor get notified when a hot lead comes in.
  • CRM lead-flow integration — LeadJig / form-to-CRM sync (Ciprian)
  • Sprint cycle alignment — SuperSymm joins LB sprint planning
  • Advisor Jetpack handoff process — coming online July
  • Attribution improvements — close the Meta pixel gap surfaced in May

May validated. June activates.

Persona pivot confirmed, winning content identified, funnel converting at $59.83 CPL. The infrastructure is in place to convert the existing lead pool while Jetpack drives net-new acquisition. $100MM AUM is the target.
